questions.description: Sophie is a property developer who is interested in purchasing and… Advise Sophie.
questions.text: Sophie is a property developer who is interested in purchasing and developing a piece of land which is owned by Jack. She approaches Jack one day and after an hour of discussions they agree that Jack will sell it to Sophie for €70,000. When Sophie asks if Jack would want a deposit, Jack says that they can "leave that sort of thing for the solicitors to sort out later". Later that evening, Sophie emails Jack the following: "Great to talk today! Am happy with the €70,000 price tag for the land. I'll get in touch with my solicitor tomorrow and get them to figure out all the formal stuff with your solicitor. Regards, Sophie" Jack immediately replies, writing: "Yes - fantastic - will chat then. Jack" However, the next day Jack contacts Sophie to say that he is not going to go ahead with the sale. He says that there was never a fully concluded agreement as they never agreed on a deposit and even if there was an agreement it was only an oral agreement and not enforceable.. Sophie sends a cheque for €70,000 to Jack in an attempt to secure the sale, but Jack does not cash it, and returns the cheque. Sophie wishes to enforce the contract. Advise Sophie.

questions.description: Oisin is the manager of a transport company, with a fleet of 50 lorries… Breach Of Contract, Company.
questions.text: Oisin is the manager of a transport company, with a fleet of 50 lorries and vans. In 2014, Oisin entered into a ten year transport contract with Supermarket Ltd, a major Irish supermarket chain. Oisin agreed to transport and deliver goods from Supermarket Ltd's warehouses to its supermarkets for a period of ten years, at a fixed price of €1 million per year. However, by 2016, political crises and unexpected changes in market conditions have caused the price of diesel to increase by 20%. By 2019 diesel prices in Ireland have increased to such an extent that Oisin is performing the contract with Supermarket Ltd at a loss. Oisin wants to know if he has any grounds on which he can terminate the transport contract with Supermarket Ltd. In addition, in September 2019 Oisin entered into a written contract with a small retail company, Shop Ltd, whereby Oisin agreed to complete all its Christmas deliveries in November and December 2019, for a set fee, payable on 1 November. However, in October 2019, Shop Ltd decided to hire a different transport company which offered a cheaper rate, and they contacted Oisin to cancel the contract. Oisin felt like he had no choice to accept the cancellation, but now wants to sue for breach of contract. Shop Ltd have stated that they were entitled to cancel the contract because they had not paid a deposit and performance had not yet begun. Advise Oisin.

questions.description: Niamh owns a commercial cleaning business. Breach Of Contract, Damages.
questions.text: Niamh owns a commercial cleaning business. She has recently entered into 3 new cleaning contracts, due to start in April, and to facilitate this she orders new specialized cleaning equipment from Ross. The contract with Ross states that this equipment will be delivered by the end of March, but Ross fails to deliver at this date. Despite numerous emails and complaints, Ross does not deliver the equipment until mid-May. Niamh is furious. The delay in getting this equipment has meant that she has had to delay the start date for 2 of her contracts, and her third client has cancelled the contract altogether. This third contract was a particularly lucrative contract with a pharmaceutical company, which would have earned her substantially more than her regular contracts. Niamh seeks damages to compensate for her financial losses and for the emotional stress the delay has caused her. Ross admits that he is in breach of contract, but refuses to pay damages to Niamh. He says that Niamh could have mitigated her losses by hiring equipment when there was a delay in delivery, and that he did not know about the contract with the pharmaceutical company as Niamh never told him about it. He also says that it's "not his problem" if the delay caused Niamh stress. Advise Niamh

questions.description: Maire is the owner of the Comfy Cafe. Breach Of Contract, Penalty Clause.
questions.text: Maire is the owner of the Comfy Cafe. She decides to expand her cafe and hires Bob to build a fully completed extension on to the existing premises. The contract between Maire and Bob clearly states that the work is to be finished by 31 January 2020, and it includes a penalty clause which says that Bob is to be liable for the loss caused by any delays, howsoever caused. Bob enters into a sub-contract with Charles, a local plumber in town. Charles agrees to carry out all the plumbing work on the extension by 31 December 2019. Bob emphasises that it is essential that this work be done on time. However, by mid-November 2019 it becomes clear that Charles will not be able to complete the work on time. Charles demands €5,000 to enable him to hire extra workers so he can finish the job on time. Bob reluctantly agrees to this and Charles manages to finish on time. However, once the work is done Bob writes Charles a letter saying that he is not going to pay Charles the extra €5,000. He says he only agreed to the extra money under duress, and that Charles "did nothing extra to earn the cash". In addition Maire is unhappy with some of the plumbing work completed by Charles. She claims that water leaks from the pipes and is causing damage to her floors. She is threatening to sue Charles for breach of contract. Advise Charles.

questions.description: In what circumstances will a party to a contract be entitled to treat itself as discharged from contractual obligations…
questions.text: In what circumstances will a party to a contract be entitled to treat itself as discharged from contractual obligations as a result of a breach of the contract by the other party?

questions.description: Outline the statutory limitations which have been put on the inclusion of exclusion clauses in consumer and commercial…
questions.text: Outline the statutory limitations which have been put on the inclusion of exclusion clauses in consumer and commercial contracts. Refer to case law in your answer.

questions.description: With reference to Irish case law, explain the difference between a penalty… Damages, Penalty Clause.
questions.text: With reference to Irish case law, explain the difference between a penalty clause and a liquidated damages clause.

questions.description: Answer (a) or (b) With reference to case law, outline the circumstances in which it is possible to declare a contract…
questions.text: Answer (a) or (b) With reference to case law, outline the circumstances in which it is possible to declare a contract void or voidable because of a common mistake of the parties. Or Consider how the law relating to either promissory estoppal or legitimate expectation have had consequences for the rules governing the formation of contract.

questions.description: Rebecca is a member of an online forum for fans of graphic novels and… Misrepresent.
questions.text: Rebecca is a member of an online forum for fans of graphic novels and comics. She decides to sell some of her comics, so on Monday morning she places the following post on the forum: "Limited Time Offers: Early Edition Limited Copy of "Spiderman" for €50 and a copy of "Deadpool" signed by Declan Shalvey for €60. If interested email Rebecca at <comicgeek@email.ie> by noon Thursday." Cormac sees the post and decides that the Spiderman comic would be a good addition to his comic book collection. He immediately sends an email to Rebecca to say he is interested in buying the comic, but he accidentally says he will pay €500 for the comic. Rebecca immediately replies saying "Yes it's a deal!" Rebecca believes she has a contract with Cormac, which he wants to enforce, but Cormac refuses to pay, saying he clearly made a mistake and never intended to offer €500. Tom also sees Rebecca's post. Tom is a big fan of the Marvel artist Declan Shalvey, so he buys the signed Deadpool comic. However, a few months later he is at a comics event in Dublin when he bumps into Declan Shalvey, who says that it is not his signature on the comic and it must be a fake. Tom has contacted Rebecca to get his money back, claiming that she misrepresented that it was a signed copy when it is not. Rebecca says she bought the comic as signed, and did not know it was a fake either. Advise Rebecca

questions.description: IreTV is an Irish television producer, which makes TV shows and series and… Breach Of Contract, Damages.
questions.text: IreTV is an Irish television producer, which makes TV shows and series and sells them on to RTE and other broadcasters. In July, IreTV hired Sinead, a very well-known and popular Irish actress, to play the lead role in a period drama Uptown Abbey. The contract specified that Sinead would have to be available for filming Mondays to Fridays from 1st September to 20th December, and a clause in the contract stated that she was also to take part in "promotion of the series Uptown Abbey through interviews and social media". In advance of recording, IreTV spent €500,000 on hiring and preparing a location for the series, as well as hiring personnel such as camera crews, engineers and costumer designers. However, after recording for the first 4 weeks Sinead failed to turn up for 4 mornings in a row with no explanation, refusing to answer her phone. She then announced n social media that once she's "finished filming this boring series about rich dead people" she's going to focus on her music career. The managers in IreTV are furious. They view this action as a breach of contract, giving them the right to terminate her contract and seek damages for wasted expenditure and /or loss of profits. Advise IreTV.

questions.description: John goes shopping in Dublin city centre every Saturday an4 regularly… Negligenc.
questions.text: John goes shopping in Dublin city centre every Saturday an4 regularly parks his car in the 'O'Connell Street car park in the centre of Dublin. There is a sign at the entrance that states "If you enter this Car park you agree to the Terms and Conditions of Parking, available at parksafe.ie/terms and conditions" John has noticed the sign on his way in, but has never checked the website to read the terms and conditions. One Saturday John parks his car in the O'Connell Street car park and pays for his ticket as normal, but when he returns to his car a large light falls from the ceiling of the car park onto his car. John sustains cuts and bruises, and the windscreen of his car is smashed. It transpires that the light fell as a result of the negligence of the car park staff. However, Carla, the manager of the car park, claims that John cannot bring an action against the owners of the car park in either contract or tort for his injuries or the damage to his car because of clause 12 of the "Terms and Conditions of Parking". Clause 12 excludes liability "in contract or tort or any other action" for "any damage to property or persons howsoever caused" while cars are left in the car park. Advise John on whether clause 12 has been incorporated into his contract, and, assuming it is incorporated, whether it can prevent him from bringing an action against the owners of the car park. Refer to consumer protection legislation in your answer.

questions.description: Ciara is the owner of a housing development in Donegal She has contracted… Advise Liam on both of these options.
questions.text: Ciara is the owner of a housing development in Donegal She has contracted with Liam to build 50 new houses. The contract between Ciara and Liam clearly states that the work is to be finished by 30th June 2020, and staged payments amounting to €5 million are to be paid to Liam. However, in March 2020 the government announces a government ban on all construction work due to the Covid-19 virus and Liam is forced to stop all work on the extension. When the ban is lifted and work recommences in mid- May it becomes clear that Liam will not be able to complete the work on time and will incur significant additional expenses in completing the work under new health and safety requirements, meaning he will suffer a loss. Liam wishes to either escape the contract entirely without liability, or to demand that Ciara pay him a higher sum of money for the work to reflect the new circumstances. Advise Liam on both of these options.

questions.description: Discuss the ways in which an offer may be terminated.
questions.text: Discuss the ways in which an offer may be terminated. Is there any way for the offeree to ensure that an offer will remain open? Refer to cases in your answer.

questions.description: With reference to relevant authority, discuss the rule in Pinnel's case and discuss whether promissory estoppel can…
questions.text: With reference to relevant authority, discuss the rule in Pinnel's case and discuss whether promissory estoppel can provide a remedy to the party (debtor) who has relied on the promise of the creditor to accept a lesser sum in satisfaction of a larger debt. Refer to cases in your answer.

questions.description: What effect does a declaration of frustration have on a contract?
questions.text: What effect does a declaration of frustration have on a contract? What happens if money has been paid or work done under the contract before the frustrating event? Refer to cases in your answer.

questions.description: Answer (a) or (b) (a) Explain the approach of the Irish courts to a situation where a person standing as surety…
questions.text: Answer (a) or (b) (a) Explain the approach of the Irish courts to a situation where a person standing as surety (guarantor) for a loan or mortgage from a bank claims they acted under the undue influence of a third party. Refer to cases in your answer. Or (b) Discuss the approach of the Irish courts to penalty clauses in contracts. Refer to cases in your answer.
